display.purpose: Page-level headings only (h1, .gb-dialog h2). Rare — one per screen.

title.purpose: Section headings, popup title bar, party/starter names.

body.purpose: Dialog copy, field labels, form text. Cap at ~42ch.

label.purpose: Captions, LCD readouts, type badges, footnotes.

shadows.name: dialog-frame
shadows.purpose: Signature GB double-border picture frame on every dialog box.

shadows.name: button-rest
shadows.purpose: Default hard offset shadow on buttons, giving a pressable card edge.

shadows.name: button-pressed
shadows.purpose: Shadow collapses to zero on :active, paired with translate(3px, 3px).

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Starter cards / party rows: shadow grows and recolors to red on hover/focus.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for screen transitions, hints, and hover lifts.

motion.name: press-instant
motion.purpose: Button press collapse — deliberately fast and un-eased, mechanical.

components.name: Primary Button (Save)
components.description: The settings page's single pill-shaped commitment button.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 9px; cursor: pointer; border-radius: 999px; border: 2px solid #101010; background: #dc0a2d; color: #ffffff; padding: 11px 26px; letter-spacing: 1px; box-shadow: 2px 2px 0 #101010; transition: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #a80822; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translate(1px, 1px); box-shadow: 1px 1px 0 #101010; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #101010; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Continue Button
components.description: Gate 'Continue' action — deals 1 damage, square GB button.
components.css: .ds-btn-continue { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.6; padding: 0.8rem 1.1rem; cursor: pointer; color: #fff; background: #c0263a; border: 3px solid #000; box-shadow: 3px 3px 0 #000; transition: transform 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-continue:hover { background: #d8324a; } .ds-btn-continue:active { transform: translate(3px, 3px); box-shadow: 0 0 #000; } .ds-btn-continue:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #dc0a2d; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Spare Button
components.description: Gate 'Spare it' action — the system's one affirmative-green button.
components.css: .ds-btn-spare { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.6; padding: 0.8rem 1.1rem; cursor: pointer; color: #fff; background: #2e8b3d; border: 3px solid #000; box-shadow: 3px 3px 0 #000; transition: transform 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-spare:hover { background: #37a349; } .ds-btn-spare:active { transform: translate(3px, 3px); box-shadow: 0 0 #000; } .ds-btn-spare:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #dc0a2d; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: GB Dialog
components.description: Signature double-bordered Game Boy dialog box used for every gate screen and settings section.
components.html:
Dodgy appeared!

Catch it, then decide.
components.css: .ds-gb-dialog { position: relative; background: #f7f7ef; color: #17181f; border: 4px solid #000; box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 4px #f7f7ef, inset 0 0 0 8px #000, 0 0 0 4px #dc0a2d; padding: 2rem 2rem 1.6rem; max-width: 640px; width: 100%; text-align: center; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.6rem; font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; } .ds-gb-dialog-title { margin: 0.6rem 0 0.2rem; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.7; color: #17181f; max-width: 26ch; } .ds-gb-dialog-body { margin: 0.2rem 0; font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.9; color: #4a4d5c; max-width: 42ch; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Settings-page number/text field — square, high-contrast focus.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" type="text" placeholder="add a site, e.g. youtube.com" />
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; max-width: 220px; padding: 9px 10px; background: #ffffff; color: #101010; border: 2px solid #101010; border-radius: 0; font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 9px; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #101010; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-input[aria-invalid='true'] { border-color: #b3001b; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #b3001b inset; }

components.name: Type Badge
components.description: Pokémon type badge — color plus text label, never color alone.
components.css: .ds-type-badge { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; font-size: 8px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; padding: 0.35rem 0.5rem; border: 2px solid #000; border-radius: 2px; line-height: 1; color: #fff; text-transform: uppercase; background: #ee8130; }

components.name: Starter/Party Card
components.description: Selectable card used for starter picks and party rows — hard shadow grows red on hover.
components.css: .ds-starter-card { font-family: 'Press Start 2P', monospace; background: #fffdf4; border: 3px solid #000; box-shadow: 4px 4px 0 #000; padding: 1rem 0.8rem; width: 200px; cursor: pointer; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; transition: transform 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1), box-shadow 0.08s cubic-bezier(0.22,0.61,0.36,1); } .ds-starter-card:hover, .ds-starter-card:focus-visible { transform: translate(-2px, -2px); box-shadow: 6px 6px 0 #dc0a2d; outline: none; } .ds-starter-card[aria-checked='true'] { box-shadow: 4px 4px 0 #dc0a2d; border-color: #dc0a2d; } .ds-starter-name { font-size: 11px; color: #17181f; }

narrative.overview: Every surface is a piece of Pokédex hardware you're looking through, not a webpage skinned to look like one. The popup is the red dex-shell itself — bezel, lens, D-pad, LCD screen. The gate and settings pages are the chunky white dialog boxes that would pop up on that hardware: double black borders, hard corners, Press Start 2P rendered at pixel sizes small enough that -webkit-font-smoothing: none and image-rendering: pixelated read as intentional, not broken. Nothing here simulates a modern app that happens to have a retro theme; the target is Gen 2/3 Pokédex UI and PMD SpriteCollab overworld sprites specifically; if a screen wouldn't plausibly render on that hardware, it doesn't belong. The tone stays playful and retro first — collectible, a little silly, fond of its own creature — with the guilt mechanic's real emotional weight sitting underneath rather than announcing itself through the visual language. Low-HP and faint states get more urgent color and pacing, never a shift into a different (grimmer, flatter) design system. This system explicitly rejects generic mobile gacha/idle-game chrome (gradient buttons, coin-shower juice, F2P App Store polish) and flat modern SaaS (soft shadows, rounded cards, clean sans-serif) — both read as a different product wearing this one's skin.
keyCharacteristics: Chunky and tactile: thick black borders, hard offset drop-shadows, buttons that visibly displace on press
Two hardware registers only — red dex-shell chrome (popup) and white GB dialog box (gate, settings) — never invent a third
Press Start 2P everywhere, at real pixel sizes (7–14px), never smoothed
LCD green as the 'screen within the screen' for flavor text, notes, and readouts
Flat by default; the only depth cue is the hard offset shadow, never blur

rules.name: The Two-Register Rule
rules.body: Every surface is either red dex-shell chrome or a white GB dialog box. A screen that tries to be both, or introduces a third background family, breaks the hardware illusion.

rules.name: The Rarity Rule
rules.body: Spare Green appears in exactly one place: the spare/forgive action. Danger red variants are reserved for damage, faint, and destructive confirmation. Reusing either color for a neutral action dilutes the meaning both are built to carry.

rules.name: The One Typeface Rule
rules.body: Press Start 2P is the only font family anywhere in the system, including fallbacks used only for a pre-load flash. Introducing a second family breaks the hardware illusion immediately.

rules.name: The No-Scale-Up Rule
rules.body: Never render Press Start 2P above ~14px. If a heading needs more visual weight, use color (Poké Red) or spacing, not size.

rules.name: The Hard-Shadow-Only Rule
rules.body: No blur-radius greater than 0 anywhere. Every shadow in this system is a flat offset, matching pixel-art rendering.

dos: Do keep every surface inside one of the two hardware registers: red dex-shell chrome or white GB dialog box.
Do use hard, non-blurred offset shadows (Npx Npx 0 <color>) for all elevation and pressed-state feedback.
Do render Press Start 2P at real pixel sizes (7–14px) with image-rendering: pixelated and no font smoothing.
Do route settings-page heading red through --pokedex-red-heading (#bb0826) whenever red text sits on the dimmed map background — plain --pokedex-red measures 3.78:1 there and fails WCAG AA.
Do use --gb-hint (#6d707c) for the smallest caption/hint text on paper, not the old #7a7d8a value, which fails 4.5:1.
Do pair LCD green background with LCD green-dark text as one unit; never split the pair.
Do provide a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
Do keep Spare Green and the 'Spare it' action as the system's one rare affirmative color.
donts: Don't introduce gradient buttons, coin-shower particle juice, or any modern F2P/gacha mobile-game chrome.
Don't let flat modern SaaS drift in: no soft blurred shadows, no rounded cards as a default shape, no clean sans-serif creeping into any new screen.
Don't scale Press Start 2P above ~14px, and don't introduce a second typeface for readability.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on any card or list row.
Don't apply background-clip: text gradients to headings; emphasis comes from Poké Red color or spacing, never a gradient.
Don't use plain --pokedex-red for text directly against --bezel or the settings map veil — use --pokedex-red-heading instead.
Don't design the UI as if it enforces anything — no punitive chrome that reads as a technical wall rather than an honest honor-system nudge.
